name: ident-user-enum
title: ident-user-enum

commands.name: ident-user-enum

arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for ident-user-enum.
arguments.raw: -user-enum  Option present in package usage line for ident-user-enum.

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# ident-user-enum -h
 ident-user-enum v1.0 ( http://pentestmonkey.net/tools/ident-user-enum )
 
 Usage: ident-user-enum.pl ip port [ port [ port ... ] ]
 
 Queries the ident service (113/TCP) to determine the OS-level user running 
 the process listening on a given TCP port.  More than one port can be supplied.

examples.command:
ident-user-enum 192.168.1.13 22 139 445
ident-user-enum v1.0 ( http://pentestmonkey.net/tools/ident-user-enum )

192.168.1.13:22 root
192.168.1.13:139    root
192.168.1.13:445    root

ident-user-enum v1.0 ( http://pentestmonkey.net/tools/ident-user-enum )

apt_metadata.description:
Query ident to determine the owner of a TCP network process
This package is a simple PERL script to query the ident service (113/TCP)
in order to determine the owner of the process listening on each TCP port of
a target system.
.
This can help to prioritise target service during a pentest (you might want
to attack services running as root first).
Alternatively, the list of usernames gathered can be used for password
guessing attacks on other network services.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use ident-user-enum because ### ident-user-enum.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `ident-user-enum.pl ip port [ port [ port ... ] ]` as documented upstream for ident-user-enum.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair ident-user-enum with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ident-user-enum --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### ident-user-enum.
